methodology: Articles sourced from Virginia-specific RSS feeds and Google News RSS, filtered for AI/technology/workforce relevance, then assessed by Claude against the scenario framework. Each article is tagged with the region it most affects and the scenario it signals toward.

signals.title: Tech Briefs: New RAMP cohort members introduce themselves
signals.signal_reading: RAMP cohort expansion and Virginia Tech library AI grants signal grassroots capability-building in historically underserved regions, suggesting distributed peer learning networks rather than metro concentration alone.

signals.title: AI pressure grows on Richmond's young workforce - Axios
signals.signal_reading: Young workers feeling AI pressure without evidence of deep training infrastructure suggests shallow displacement rather than capability development—neither depth nor broad distribution materializing in Richmond's workforce.

signals.title: AI trainer says workers must lead technology, not follow it - WDBJ7
signals.signal_reading: AI trainer advocating worker-led technology adoption in Roanoke signals potential for deep, distributed capability if training translates to genuine fluency rather than platform dependence.

signals.title: New partnership between VCU and AI Ready RVA aims to boost AI education, innovation and community impact - VCU News
signals.signal_reading: VCU-AI Ready RVA partnership concentrates institutional resources and expertise in Richmond metro, deepening hub capability but success depends on whether knowledge diffuses beyond the university-nonprofit ecosystem.

signals.title: Hampton Roads multiple listing service introduces AI tool to personalize home searches - pilotonline.com
signals.signal_reading: Real estate MLS deploying turnkey AI search tools represents platform-delivered adoption—broad accessibility across Hampton Roads realtors but shallow engagement that doesn't build underlying technical capacity.
